prompt: I want to optimize my DSPy ticket classifier using VizPy. I have 100 labeled tickets with categories: billing, technical, account, security.
expected_output: A VizPy ContraPromptOptimizer setup for classification with proper metric, evaluation before/after, and save step
assertions: uses vizpy.ContraPromptOptimizer (not PromptGradOptimizer) since this is a classification task
sets vizpy.api_key or mentions VIZPY_API_KEY environment variable
includes a metric function comparing predicted vs expected labels
includes dspy.Evaluate on a held-out devset to verify improvement
calls optimized.save() to persist the result

prompt: I want to improve my DSPy summarizer. Should I use VizPy or GEPA?
expected_output: A comparison recommending PromptGradOptimizer for generation tasks, with GEPA as the open-source alternative, and guidance on when each is better
assertions: mentions GEPA as the open-source alternative that tunes the same thing (instructions only)
mentions MIPROv2 if they also want few-shot demo optimization
asks about data privacy since VizPy sends data to external servers
does not recommend ContraPromptOptimizer for this generation task

prompt: Set up VizPy to optimize my DSPy program and compare the results with GEPA
expected_output: Side-by-side optimization with VizPy and GEPA, with evaluation comparing both on the same devset
assertions: shows both vizpy optimizer and dspy.GEPA compile calls
uses the same metric and trainset for both
evaluates both optimized programs on the same devset for fair comparison
mentions that both only tune instructions (same optimization target)
